location: 00 AM Campus Tour 5500 Union Church Rd Flowery Branch,
description: Eagle Ranch The Lodge Tuesday, March 3 9:00 - 10:00 AM 9:00 - 9:30 AM Presentation 9:30 - 10:00 AM Campus Tour 5500 Union Church Rd Flowery Branch, GA 30542 For 40 years Eagle Ranch has brought hope and healing to children and families in crisis. Come hear Executive Director John Shackelford share how their innovative whole-family approach is providing housing, education, and counseling to equip parents and children with powerful tools to restore relationships and rebuild trust. It’s a meaningful opportunity for local business leaders to learn, be encouraged, and explore how we can come alongside this mission. title: Small Business Seminar - How The UGA Small Business Development Center Can Help You Thrive in 2026!
description: Tuesday, March 10th Networking 8:15 - 8:30 AM Seminar 8:30 - 9:30 AM Join Tom Jones and Stephanie Sokenis to find out about the tools and resources available through your local UGA SBDC office to help you tackle the biggest challenges in your mature or startup business. One of the best small business resources you may not know about, the SBDC offers no-cost one-on-one consulting and a variety of training opportunities that support informed decision-making and long-term growth. During this session, attendees will learn how a business can use the SBDC to: Work one-on-one with a consultant at no cost to identify challenges, clarify goals, and plan next steps Use strategic planning to support decisions, growth initiatives, and operational improvements Analyze P&L, cash flow or capital acquisition opportunities Implement effective marketing strategies, digital marketing initiatives, and market research Enhance operations and efficiency, including systems, processes, and staffing considerations Powered in part by the U.S. Small Business Administration, the University of Georgia Small Business Development Center combines no-cost consultations and a variety of training workshops and classe

title: Health & Wellness Seminar - Leading the Way in Neuroscience and Neurosurgical Care at NGHS
description: Michael Glanzman, Executive Director of Neurosciences for Northeast Georgia Health System and Dr. Zain Boghani, neurosurgeon with Northeast Georgia Physicians Group will offer a high-level overview of the Neuroscience service line at NGHS, featuring its comprehensive clinical programs and specialized teams. They will then dive deeper into the Neurosurgery program, exploring surgical treatment options and the seamless, coordinated care that defines the patient experience. Together, they will highlight how their multidisciplinary teams work together to serve and support communities throughout the region. Michael Glanzman, Executive Director of Neurosciences for Northeast Georgia Health System Zain Boghani, MD, neurosurgeon with Northeast Georgia Physicians Group If you would like to attend on zoom, please register via the registration button. An email will be sent prior to the seminar with a zoom link. 230 E.E. Butler Parkway Gainesville, GA 30501 | Phone: (770) 532-6206

title: Small Business Seminar - Future-Ready Hall County
description: Tuesday, April 14th Networking 8:15 - 8:30 AM Seminar 8:30 - 9:30 AM Future-Ready Hall County helps small business owners move from AI curiosity to AI confidence. In this engaging session, Renee Hayes and Tai Anderson introduce the basics of AI and demonstrate accessible tools that can drive growth, increase efficiency, and sharpen decision-making. Walk away with practical steps to future-proof your North Georgia business. Renee Hayes, MBA, is the Executive Director of Evergreen Learning at the University of North Georgia, where she led the successful launch of the university’s newest venture into lifelong learning & Tai Anderson, a unique voice in the speaking world, known for his innovative fusion of musical artistry and marketing acumen as a founding member of the Grammy-winning band Third Day. As Creative Director for Supreme Lending Southeast and an Evergreen Learning Instructor at the University of North Georgia, Tai brings those lessons from the stage into the business world. Through Continuing Education courses and keynote presentations, he helps professionals understand the power of brand storytelling, fan-driven marketing, and meaningful audience engagement 8:15 - 8:30 AM

title: South Hall Business Coalition - King's Hawaiian
description: King's Hawaiian Tuesday, April 21 9:00 - 10:00 AM 5425 Aloha Way Flowery Branch, GA 30542 Join us for an update and tour of the King's Hawaiian Bakery in South Hall County, where their flagship product, Hawaiian sweet bread, is produced. Tour attendees will see a state-of-the-art facility and learn what it takes to produce the irresistible sweet rolls from start to finish. Founded in 1955, the company has grown from a small bakery operation in Hawaii, to a distribution company selling bread, rolls, and pretzels bites nationwide. The family-owned food company expanded its Oakwood facility, creating more than 160 new jobs. This new 150,000-square-foot bakery is located across the street from the King's Hawaiian facility in Oakwood South Industrial Park. King's Hawaiian opened in Oakwood in 2010, and today provides over 800 jobs for the Hall County community. **Please note: space is LIMITED. Max 3 people per organization** REGISTER HERE $8.00 Member $15.00 Non-Member Light breakfast included 230 E.E. Butler Parkway Gainesville, GA 30501 | Phone: (770) 532-6206

title: Health & Wellness Seminar - Assistive Technology: For a person without disabilities, technology makes LIFE easier...For a person with disabilities, technology makes LIFE possible!
description: The world around us is always inventing! Emily Major with LegacyLink stays up to date with the latest technologies that may be able to assist those with disabilities, limitations, or anyone who is simply curious as to what possibilities are out there. Technology does not have to be “high-tech” either, it could be as simple as a magnifying glass or an adaptive shoelace. You never know what someone will invent next! Emily has worked at Legacy Link as their Program Monitor and Assistive Technology Coordinator for 6 years. She is passionate about helping adults age in place, advocates for a healthy aging population, and encourages clients to voice their concerns to promote positive change. 230 E.E. Butler Parkway Gainesville, GA 30501 | Phone: (770) 532-6206

title: 118th Annual Gala
description: A Celebration of Legacy & Leadership Presented by the Board of Directors of the Greater Hall Chamber of Commerce 118th Annual Meeting & Gala Thursday, May 7, 2026 6:00 p.m. - 9:00 p.m. The Boathouse at Lake Lanier Olympic Park We invite you to a lakeside evening of celebration marking the Chamber’s 118th year and honoring the leaders and businesses that shape our community. Set against the beautiful backdrop of Lake Lanier, this signature event features the presentation of Annual Business Awards, recognizing outstanding small and family-owned businesses, along with individual honors for exceptional leadership and distinguished service. The evening will also include the Passing of the Gavel, as we welcome our incoming executive leadership and usher in a new year for the Greater Hall Chamber. Black Tie Optional Sunset Cocktail Reception 6:00 p.m. Dinner & Program 7:30 p.m. $200 per person | $1,600 Table (seats 8) Sponsorships: Platinum $3,500 | Gold $2,500 6:00 PM- Outdoor Reception | 7:30 PM- Dinner and Program 230 E.E. Butler Parkway Gainesville, GA 30501 | Phone: (770) 532-6206
